description: 66,041 raw_mentions reference .txt files (text/plain) but have no download_status set — they were silently dropped by the Telegram connector's 100MB size limit without even getting queued. Once da-38e raises the limit to 5GB, these need to be retroactively marked pending so download_pending_files picks them up. Priority target: mansory*.txt files (3.4k-3.9k copies each) which are stolen credential logs. Also 10,940 .txt mentions already marked pending but never downloaded — these should start flowing once the size limit is raised. Query to backfill: UPDATE raw_mentions SET metadata = jsonb_set(metadata, '{download_status}', '"pending"') WHERE metadata->>'file_name' LIKE '%.txt' AND metadata->>'download_status' IS NULL AND metadata->>'file_size' IS NOT NULL;
title: Backfill download_status=pending on 66k .txt mentions stuck with null status

MAX_DOWNLOAD_SIZE is hardcoded at 100MB in telegram.py:45. User never requested this limit. Stealer log archives (Trident Cloud etc, 884MB-3.8GB) are being skipped. Make the limit configurable via the settings UI, stored in DB. Default to 5GB. Also check max_archive_size (100MB) in stealer_log.py:14 and align it. The limit was introduced in fe38112 (Mar 14) as a polecat defensive measure.
title: Configurable download size limit: UI setting, default 5GB

description: When a zip file contains inner zip/rar/7z archives, only the outer archive is extracted. Need recursive extraction with depth limit. The stealer_log connector has _extract_archive() but it's single-level. Should extract nested archives and store all files in S3 with parent/child relationships.
title: Nested archive extraction: recursively extract archives within archives

description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-azz2h
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-21T18:45:32Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Integrate a comprehensive BIN (Bank Identification Number) database into darkdisco for identifying card issuers from BIN prefixes found in CC dump findings.

Architecture:
1. New bin_database table: bin_prefix (varchar, indexed), bin_range_start, bin_range_end (for range-based lookups), issuer_name, card_brand (Visa/Mastercard/Amex/Discover/etc), card_type (credit/debit/prepaid), card_level (classic/gold/platinum/business), country_code, country_name, bank_url, bank_phone, source (which dataset it came from), updated_at
2. Data ingestion pipeline:
   - Parse Visa BIN range PDFs (Visa publishes BIN Table updates)
   - Parse Mastercard BIN range PDFs (Mastercard publishes similar)
   - Import from open BIN databases (binlist.net API as fallback, or static CSV datasets)
   - Management command: bd bin-import --source visa --file visa_bins.pdf
   - Management command: bd bin-import --source csv --file bins.csv
3. BIN lookup API: GET /api/bins/lookup/{prefix} returns issuer info
4. Auto-enrichment: when a finding contains BIN-matchable numbers (6-8 digit prefixes), auto-lookup and annotate the finding metadata with issuer info for all detected BINs
5. Frontend: BIN lookup tool page, and BIN issuer display in finding detail when card numbers are detected
6. PDF parsing: use pdfplumber or camelot to extract BIN range tables from Visa/MC PDFs. Handle both static prefix lists and range formats (e.g., 400000-400099 = Bank X).

Modern BIN standard is 8 digits (IIN), but legacy 6-digit prefixes still in wide use. Support both.
title: BIN database integration for card issuer identification

extract_archive() (in-memory path) recurses into nested archives up to _MAX_ARCHIVE_DEPTH. But stream_extract_from_s3() (disk-based path for large files, line 341 in files.py) does NOT recurse — it just os.walks flat output. Nested zips inside large stealer log archives are listed as files but never opened. Need to add recursive extraction to the streaming path. Related: da-3om was filed for this but never started.
title: Recursive extraction in stream_extract_from_s3 for nested archives

close_reason: Merged to main (864d0c8). File storage, archives endpoint, duplicate sub-files fix.
description:
attached_molecule: da-wisp-duwku
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-28T09:14:52Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Major data regression in file download/extraction/display pipeline:
1. Files we previously had (e.g. cvv190_cloud zips) no longer show up in search at all — data loss
2. Archives that ARE listed show the same sub-files across different archives (display/indexing bug)
3. Nested archives still not being extracted and viewable (da-3om was filed but not completed)

This is a P0 data integrity issue. The fix must:
- Recover or re-index missing file data
- Fix archive sub-file listing to show correct per-archive contents
- Implement recursive archive extraction (or fix if broken)
- Add safeguards to prevent data loss during future code changes (e.g. migration checks, file count assertions before/after deploys)

Previously had cvv190_cloud zips extracted with viewable content — this must be restored.
notes:
Root cause analysis:

BUG 1 - Files missing from search:
_store_extracted_files() (worker.py:536) only creates ExtractedFile rows from extracted_file_contents in metadata, which only includes TEXT files (worker.py:424-434). Non-text files (images, binaries) are uploaded to S3 but get no DB row. The search endpoint queries ExtractedFile table, so non-text files are invisible.

BUG 2 - Archives showing duplicate sub-files:
/extracted-files endpoint returns individual ExtractedFile rows. Frontend loadArchives() treats each row as an 'archive' in the left panel. Multiple rows from the same archive share the same mention_id, so clicking any of them loads the same archive-contents via /mentions/{mention_id}/archive-contents. Need a proper archives-summary endpoint.

BUG 3 - Nested archives not extracted:
In-memory extraction has nested support, but _process_file_mentions only stores text file info in extracted_file_contents metadata. The extract_stored_archive backfill task correctly creates rows for ALL files but skips mentions that already have some ExtractedFile rows.

FIX PLAN:
1. Modify _process_file_mentions to store ALL extracted file metadata (not just text)
2. Modify _store_extracted_files to create rows for ALL files (matching extract_stored_archive behavior)
3. Add /archives endpoint grouping by mention_id
4. Fix frontend to use archives endpoint for left panel
5. Add reindex task for existing data
title: REGRESSION: Files missing from search, archives showing duplicate sub-files, no nested extraction

Build a comprehensive test suite for darkdisco. Priorities: (1) Matching pipeline: raw mention -> watch term matching -> fraud indicator check -> negative pattern suppression -> finding creation. Test that negative patterns correctly suppress spam, fraud indicators gate weak signals, strong signals (BIN, routing) bypass filters. (2) Enrichment pipeline: dedup scoring, FP detection, threat intel boosting, severity adjustment. (3) Finding lifecycle: status transitions, analyst notes, audit log. (4) Report generation: verify all new sections (FP analytics, pattern stats, threat summary, analyst performance) render correctly. (5) Alert rules: trigger evaluation, notification dispatch. (6) API tests: all CRUD endpoints, institution management, watch terms, retroactive hunt. Use pytest with existing docker-compose stack.
title: End-to-end test suite for matching, enrichment, and reporting pipeline

Worker logs show successful Telegram polls (5966 mentions from 333 channels) but raw_mentions table has no rows after Mar 24. Last ingestion: Mar 24 (55,936 rows). The poll_source task succeeds but mentions aren't being written to the database. Likely a persistence/commit issue in the poll->save pipeline. Also seeing Trapline Integration poll failures (400 Bad Request on /api/v1/watchlist/bins) but that's a separate issue. Primary focus: fix the mention persistence gap.
notes: Root cause: raw_mention persistence is inside the matching loop, gated by (1) watch term existence and (2) Finding dedup check. Two paths lose data: (1) no watch terms → early return at line 607 before persistence code (2) duplicate Finding hash → continue at line 648 skips raw_mention save. Fix: persist all raw_mentions unconditionally BEFORE watch term/dedup checks.
title: Telegram mentions polled but not persisted to DB since Mar 25

description:
cvv190_cloud.zip files (and likely all file attachments) exist in raw_mentions metadata (file_name, file_size, file_mime) but were never downloaded to S3/extracted_files. Root cause: raw_mentions table has no download_status column — the download worker has no way to find mentions with downloadable files. 

Previous bead da-386 identified this gap (66k .txt mentions) but was never completed.

Required:
1. Add download_status column to raw_mentions (pending/downloading/complete/failed/skipped)
2. Backfill existing mentions: set download_status=pending where metadata has file_name/has_media=true
3. Fix download worker to query by download_status=pending
4. Verify cvv190_cloud files get downloaded and appear in extracted_files
5. Verify files show up in the UI search

This is blocking client report — these are key stealer log archives.
title: File download pipeline not processing mentions — missing download_status column and tracking
